summary: Ran backend pytest regression and mobile-first Expo web integration checks for the optional top-down 3-plane cube hex renderer style across game modes and website flow. All requested flows passed.
action_items: Optional hardening: add stable testID/data-testid hooks for web iframe fallback and injected adapter controls to improve automation resilience.
Optional config alignment: frontend currently uses EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL; platform guideline references EXPO_BACKEND_URL.
critical_code_review_comments: No backend API regressions reproduced in this run.
Top-down 3-plane game render contract is present and exercised across invaders/platformer/maze.
Renderer adapter injection in server.py is large and tightly coupled; consider modularizing for maintainability and safer future changes.
seed_data_creation: Created TEST_ render record via POST /api/renders during pytest persistence check.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Validated on public URL https://hex-palette-render.preview.emergentagent.com with 375x667 viewport. Verified game mode counts: invaders 49 cells/10 tokens, platformer 49/4, maze 43/3; each cell has top/left/bottom faces; cubeSize 48 updates cell width to 48px live; calibrated wheel update recolors ground + token colors; website render mode still works and /api/health returns 200.
rca_of_issue: No feature failure reproduced in this iteration. One temporary test failure occurred due to overly strict static-string assertion (expected runtime class combination text); fixed by asserting stylesheet/runtime contract selectors instead.
